description: This figure presents comprehensive TEM characterization of precipitates in Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steels, comparing Ti/V-free (M1) and Ti/V-modified (M2) alloys after aging at 550°C. Panels (a–c) show BF, DF, and SAED (ZA=[011]) images of sample M1-30, revealing a high density of uniformly distributed intragranular κ-carbides throughout the austenite matrix. Panels (d–f) for M2-10 display BF, DF, and SAED (ZA=[1̄10]) images demonstrating the coexistence of κ-carbides and pre-existing (Ti,V)C carbides, where κ-carbides preferentially precipitate heterogeneously at the edges of (Ti,V)C particles with almost no matrix precipitation. Panels (g–j) for M2-30 present BF, CDF images of both phases, and SAED (ZA=[100]), confirming that after 30 h aging the κ-carbides exhibit increased number density and larger sizes but remain heterogeneously distributed at (Ti,V)C edges. The accompanying table reports quantitative precipitate data: M1-30 contains only κ-carbides (volume fraction 7.4±3.4%, mean radius 10.8±6.2 nm), whereas M2-30 contains both (Ti,V)C (2.3±1.7%, 37.1±22.6 nm) and κ-carbides (3.2±2.8%, 9.7±8.5 nm). These results establish that (Ti,V)C carbides fundamentally alter κ-carbide precipitation behavior by providing heterogeneous nucleation sites, shifting the distribution from uniform intragranular to localized interphase precipitation.
